title.raw: 《陳丞相世家》 House of Chancellor Chen

sentences.zh: 陳丞相平者，陽武戶牖鄉人也。

translations.literal: Prime Minister Chen Ping was a native of Huyou village in Yangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Chen Ping was a native of Huyou Township in the district of Yangwu.

sentences.zh: 少時家貧，好讀書，有田三十畝，獨與兄伯居。

translations.literal: In his youth his family was poor, but he loved reading books. He had thirty mu of fields and lived only with his elder brother Bo.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth, his family was poor, yet he loved to read. He owned just thirty mu of farmland and lived alone with his elder brother Bo.

sentences.zh: 伯常耕田，縱平使游學。

translations.literal: Bo constantly tilled the fields, allowing Ping to travel and study.
translations.idiomatic: Bo worked the fields without cease, letting Ping roam freely in pursuit of learning.

sentences.zh: 平為人長［大］美色。

translations.literal: Ping was tall and handsome in appearance.
translations.idiomatic: Ping was tall, imposing, and strikingly handsome.

sentences.zh: 人或謂陳平曰：「貧何食而肥若是？」

translations.literal: Someone said to Chen Ping: 'Though poor, how do you eat to become so plump?'
translations.idiomatic: Someone once asked Chen Ping, "You are so poor—what do you eat to look so well fed?"

sentences.zh: 其嫂嫉平之不視家生產，曰：「亦食糠覈耳。

translations.literal: His sister-in-law resented that Ping did not oversee the family production and said: 'He also eats only bran and husks.
translations.idiomatic: His sister-in-law, who resented Ping for never tending to the family livelihood, snapped, "He eats nothing but chaff and husks.

sentences.zh: 有叔如此，不如無有。」

translations.literal: Having an uncle like this, it would be better to have none.'
translations.idiomatic: Better to have no brother-in-law at all than one like him."

sentences.zh: 伯聞之，逐其婦而棄之。

translations.literal: Bo heard this and drove away his wife, divorcing her.
translations.idiomatic: When Bo heard this, he drove his wife out and divorced her.

sentences.zh: 及平長，可娶妻，富人莫肯與者，貧者平亦恥之。

translations.literal: When Ping grew up and was of marriageable age, rich families would not give their daughters to him, and Ping also disdained poor families.
translations.idiomatic: When Ping came of age to marry, no wealthy family would give him a daughter, and Ping for his part was too proud to marry into a poor one.

sentences.zh: 久之，戶牖富人有張負，張負女孫五嫁而夫輒死，人莫敢娶。

translations.literal: After a long time, there was a rich man in Huyou named Zhang Fu. Zhang Fu's granddaughter had married five times, and each husband died, so no one dared marry her.
translations.idiomatic: After some time, there was a wealthy man in Huyou named Zhang Fu whose granddaughter had been married five times, and every husband had died—so no one dared take her as a wife.

sentences.zh: 平欲得之。

translations.literal: Ping desired her.
translations.idiomatic: Ping wished to marry her.

sentences.zh: 邑中有喪，平貧，侍喪，以先往後罷為助。

translations.literal: There was a funeral in the town. Ping was poor and attended the funeral, helping by arriving early and leaving late.
translations.idiomatic: When there was a funeral in the village, Ping, too poor to offer gifts, helped instead by arriving first and leaving last.

sentences.zh: 張負既見之喪所，獨視偉平，平亦以故後去。

translations.literal: Zhang Fu saw him at the funeral site and gazed admiringly at Ping alone. Ping also deliberately left late.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fu noticed him at the funeral and watched Ping with admiration. Ping, for his part, deliberately lingered before leaving.

sentences.zh: 負隨平至其家，家乃負郭窮巷，以獘席為門，然門外多有長者車轍。

translations.literal: Fu followed Ping to his home. The home was in a poor alley outside the city walls, with a tattered mat as a door, yet outside the door there were many ruts from noblemen's carriages.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fu followed Ping home. The dwelling stood in a poor lane just outside the city wall, with nothing but a ragged mat for a door—yet the ground outside was rutted with the tracks of distinguished visitors' carriages.

sentences.zh: 張負歸，謂其子仲曰：「吾欲以女孫予陳平。」

translations.literal: Zhang Fu returned and told his son Zhong: 'I wish to give my granddaughter to Chen Ping.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fu returned home and told his son Zhong, "I intend to give my granddaughter in marriage to Chen Ping."

sentences.zh: 張仲曰：「平貧不事事，一縣中盡笑其所為，獨柰何予女乎？」

translations.literal: Zhang Zhong said: "Ping is poor and does nothing. The entire county laughs at what he does—why alone would you give him your daughter?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Zhong replied, "Ping is poor and does nothing useful. The whole county laughs at him—why on earth would you give him your granddaughter?"

sentences.zh: 負曰：「人固有好美如陳平而長貧賤者乎？」

translations.literal: Fu said: "Are there people who are as fond of beauty and as handsome as Chen Ping yet remain poor and lowly forever?"
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fu answered, "Has there ever been a man as fine-looking as Chen Ping who stayed poor and lowly forever?"

sentences.zh: 卒與女。

translations.literal: In the end he gave him his daughter.
translations.idiomatic: In the end, he gave his granddaughter to Ping.

sentences.zh: 為平貧，乃假貸幣以聘，予酒肉之資以內婦。

translations.literal: Because Ping was poor, he lent money for the betrothal gifts and provided funds for wine and meat to bring in the bride.
translations.idiomatic: Because Ping was poor, Zhang Fu lent him money for the betrothal gifts and provided the funds for the wedding feast so he could bring home his bride.

sentences.zh: 負誡其孫曰：「毋以貧故，事人不謹。

translations.literal: Fu admonished his granddaughter: "Do not because of poverty serve people carelessly.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Fu admonished his granddaughter, "Do not let poverty make you careless in serving others.

sentences.zh: 事兄伯如事父，事嫂如母。」

translations.literal: Serve elder brother Bo as you would a father, serve sister-in-law as you would a mother."
translations.idiomatic: Serve your brother-in-law Bo as you would your own father, and serve your sister-in-law as you would your own mother."

sentences.zh: 平既娶張氏女，齎用益饒，游道日廣。

translations.literal: After Ping married the Zhang family daughter, his resources became increasingly abundant, and his social connections grew daily.
translations.idiomatic: After Ping married into the Zhang family, his means grew more abundant and his circle of acquaintances expanded by the day.

sentences.zh: 裏中社，平為宰，分肉食甚均。

translations.literal: At the village sacrificial feast, Ping served as steward and distributed the meat very evenly.
translations.idiomatic: At the village sacrificial feast, Ping served as the steward and divided the sacrificial meat with perfect fairness.

sentences.zh: 父老曰：「善，陳孺子之為宰！」

translations.literal: The elders said: "Excellent! How Chen the young man serves as steward!"
translations.idiomatic: The village elders exclaimed, "Splendid! What a fine steward young Chen makes!"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「嗟乎，使平得宰天下，亦如是肉矣！」

translations.literal: Ping said: "Alas, if Ping could govern the realm, he would also distribute meat like this!"
translations.idiomatic: Ping sighed, "Ah, if only I could govern all under heaven, I would apportion it just as I have this meat!"

sentences.zh: 陳涉起而王陳，使周市略定魏地，立魏咎為魏王，與秦軍相攻於臨濟。

translations.literal: Chen She rose up and became king of Chen, sent Zhou Shi to pacify and seize Wei territory, established Wei Jiu as King of Wei, and attacked Qin armies at Linji.
translations.idiomatic: Chen She rose in rebellion and declared himself King of Chen, dispatching Zhou Shi to subjugate the old Wei territories. He installed Wei Jiu as King of Wei, who then clashed with the Qin armies at Linji.

sentences.zh: 陳平固已前謝其兄伯，從少年往事魏王咎於臨濟。

translations.literal: Chen Ping had already previously taken leave of his elder brother Bo and followed young men to serve King Wei Jiu at Linji.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping had already taken leave of his brother Bo and joined a band of young men to serve King Wei Jiu at Linji.

sentences.zh: 魏王以為太仆。

translations.literal: The King of Wei appointed him Grand Coachman.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Wei appointed him Grand Coachman.

sentences.zh: 說魏王不聽，人或讒之，陳平亡去。

translations.literal: He advised the King of Wei but was not listened to. Someone slandered him, so Chen Ping fled.
translations.idiomatic: He offered counsel to the King of Wei, but was ignored. When someone slandered him at court, Chen Ping fled.

sentences.zh: 久之，項羽略地至河上，陳平往歸之，從入破秦，賜平爵卿。

translations.literal: After a long time, Xiang Yu conquered territory to the Yellow River. Chen Ping went to join him, followed him in defeating Qin, and Xiang Yu granted Ping the rank of noble.
translations.idiomatic: Some time later, Xiang Yu swept through the territory as far as the Yellow River. Chen Ping went to join him, followed him into the campaign that destroyed Qin, and was rewarded with the noble rank of minister.

sentences.zh: 項羽之東王彭城也，漢王還定三秦而東，殷王反楚。

translations.literal: When Xiang Yu became king of Pengcheng in the east, the Han King returned to pacify the Three Qins and went east. Yin Wang rebelled against Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Yu went east to rule from Pengcheng, the King of Han returned to pacify the Three Qin regions and then marched east. The King of Yin revolted against Chu.

sentences.zh: 項羽乃以平為信武君，將魏王咎客在楚者以往，擊降殷王而還。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu then appointed Ping as Lord Xinwu, commanding the guests of King Wei Jiu who were in Chu to go and attack, subduing Yin Wang and returning.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu thereupon named Ping Lord of Xinwu, placed him in command of the former retainers of King Wei Jiu who had remained in Chu, and sent them to subdue the King of Yin. They forced his surrender and returned.

sentences.zh: 項王使項悍拜平為都尉，賜金二十溢。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu sent Xiang Han to appoint Ping as Commandant, granting gold worth twenty yi.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang dispatched Xiang Han to formally appoint Ping as Commandant and bestow upon him twenty measures of gold.

sentences.zh: 居無何，漢王攻下殷（王）。

translations.literal: Soon after, the Han King attacked and conquered Yin.
translations.idiomatic: Before long, the King of Han attacked and conquered the territory of Yin.

sentences.zh: 項王怒，將誅定殷者將吏。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu was angry and intended to execute the generals and officers who had conquered Yin.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang was furious and resolved to execute the generals and officers who had been responsible for pacifying Yin.

sentences.zh: 陳平懼誅，乃封其金與印，使使歸項王，而平身閒行杖劍亡。

translations.literal: Chen Ping feared execution, so he sealed his gold and seal and sent an envoy to return them to Xiang Yu, while Ping himself slipped away carrying a sword.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping, fearing execution, sealed up his gold and official seal and sent a messenger to return them to the King of Xiang. Then, carrying only his sword, he slipped away alone.

sentences.zh: 渡河，船人見其美丈夫獨行，疑其亡將，要中當有金玉寶器，目之，欲殺平。

translations.literal: He crossed the river. The boatman saw this handsome man traveling alone, suspected he was a fleeing general, and thought there must be gold and jade treasures in his middle, eyed him, and wanted to kill Ping.
translations.idiomatic: When he came to cross the Yellow River, the boatman saw this tall, handsome man traveling alone and suspected he was a deserting general whose belt surely concealed gold, jade, and other treasures. The boatman eyed him hungrily and wanted to kill him.

sentences.zh: 平恐，乃解衣躶而佐刺船，船人知其無有，乃止。

translations.literal: Ping was afraid, so he stripped naked and helped pole the boat. The boatman knew he had nothing and stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Alarmed, Ping stripped off his clothes and, naked, helped pole the boat. When the boatman saw he carried nothing of value, he abandoned his designs.

sentences.zh: 平遂至修武降漢，因魏無知求見漢王，漢王召入。

translations.literal: Ping then reached Xiuwu and surrendered to Han, requesting through Wei Wuzhi to see the Han King. The Han King summoned him in.
translations.idiomatic: Ping made his way to Xiuwu and surrendered to Han. Through Wei Wuzhi he secured an audience with the King of Han, who summoned him inside.

sentences.zh: 是時萬石君奮為漢王中涓，受平謁，入見平。

translations.literal: At this time Wan Shi Jun Fen was the Han King's attendant and received Ping's visit, bringing him in to see Ping.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Shi Fen, later known as Lord Wanshi, was serving as the King of Han's personal attendant. He received Ping's request for an audience and ushered him in.

sentences.zh: 平等七人俱進，賜食。

translations.literal: The seven of them all advanced and were granted food.
translations.idiomatic: Ping and his six companions all came forward and were given a meal.

sentences.zh: 王曰：「罷，就舍矣。」

translations.literal: The king said: "Dismissed, go to your quarters."
translations.idiomatic: The king said, "You may retire to your quarters now."

sentences.zh: 平曰：「臣為事來，所言不可以過今日。」

translations.literal: Ping said: "Your servant has come for matters of importance. What I have to say cannot wait beyond today."
translations.idiomatic: Ping replied, "I have come on a matter of urgency. What I wish to say cannot wait another day."

sentences.zh: 於是漢王與語而說之，問曰：「子之居楚何官？」

translations.literal: Thus the Han King spoke with him and was pleased. He asked: "What position did you hold in Chu?"
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han then spoke with him privately and was delighted. He asked, "What office did you hold when you served Chu?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「為都尉。」

translations.literal: He said: "Commandant."
translations.idiomatic: Ping answered, "I served as Commandant."

sentences.zh: 是日乃拜平為都尉，使為參乘，典護軍。

translations.literal: That day he appointed Ping as Commandant, had him ride as chariot companion, and oversee the army guards.
translations.idiomatic: That very day the king appointed Ping as Commandant, made him ride as his chariot companion, and charged him with overseeing the military guard.

sentences.zh: 諸將盡讙，曰：「大王一日得楚之亡卒，未知其高下，而即與同載，反使監護軍長者！」

translations.literal: All the generals jeered, saying: "The Great King has gotten a deserter from Chu in one day, not knowing his worth, yet immediately rides with him and even has him oversee the senior generals!"
translations.idiomatic: The generals were all in an uproar. "The Great King picks up a deserter from Chu," they grumbled, "knows nothing of his merit or character, yet rides in the same chariot with him and even sets him over the senior officers!"

sentences.zh: 漢王聞之，愈益幸平。

translations.literal: The Han King heard this and trusted Ping all the more.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han heard their complaints, he only trusted and favored Ping the more.

sentences.zh: 遂與東伐項王。

translations.literal: He then went east to attack Xiang Yu.
translations.idiomatic: He then set out eastward with Ping to campaign against the King of Xiang.

sentences.zh: 至彭城，為楚所敗。

translations.literal: They reached Pengcheng and were defeated by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: They reached Pengcheng, where Chu dealt them a crushing defeat.

sentences.zh: 引而還，收散兵至滎陽，以平為亞將，屬於韓王信，軍廣武。

translations.literal: They led the retreat, gathered scattered troops at Xingyang, appointed Ping as deputy general under King Han Xin, and stationed at Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: They retreated, rallying scattered soldiers as they fell back to Xingyang. Ping was appointed deputy general under King Han Xin of Han, and the army encamped at Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 絳侯、灌嬰等咸讒陳平曰：「平雖美丈夫，如冠玉耳，其中未必有也。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Jiang, Guan Ying and others all slandered Chen Ping, saying: "Ping, though a handsome man, is like a jade crown—beautiful outside but may have nothing inside.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Jiang, Guan Ying, and others all spoke against Chen Ping: "Ping may be a handsome fellow, but he is like jade on a cap—all show on the outside, with nothing of substance within.

sentences.zh: 臣聞平居家時，盜其嫂；

translations.literal: We have heard that when Ping lived at home, he stole his sister-in-law;
translations.idiomatic: We have heard that while he lived at home, he had an affair with his sister-in-law;

sentences.zh: 事魏不容，亡歸楚；

translations.literal: Served Wei but was not tolerated, fled and joined Chu;
translations.idiomatic: that he served Wei but was cast out, and fled to Chu;

sentences.zh: 歸楚不中，又亡歸漢。

translations.literal: Did not fit in Chu, fled again and joined Han.
translations.idiomatic: that he did not fit in at Chu either, and fled again to Han.

sentences.zh: 今日大王尊官之，令護軍。

translations.literal: Now the Great King honors him with high office and has him oversee the army.
translations.idiomatic: Now the Great King has honored him with high office and set him over the army.

sentences.zh: 臣聞平受諸將金，金多者得善處，金少者得惡處。

translations.literal: We have heard Ping accepts gold from the generals—those who give much gold get good positions, those who give little get bad positions.
translations.idiomatic: We hear that Ping accepts gold from the generals—those who give generously receive favorable assignments, while those who give little are sent to the worst posts.

sentences.zh: 平，反覆亂臣也，願王察之。」

translations.literal: Ping is a treacherous minister of repeated betrayals. We hope the king will investigate him."
translations.idiomatic: Ping is a faithless, disloyal minister. We beg Your Majesty to look into the matter."

sentences.zh: 漢王疑之，召讓魏無知。

translations.literal: The Han King doubted him and summoned to rebuke Wei Wuzhi.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han grew suspicious and summoned Wei Wuzhi to reprimand him.

sentences.zh: 無知曰：「臣所言者，能也；

translations.literal: Wei Wuzhi said: "What I spoke of was his ability;
translations.idiomatic: Wei Wuzhi replied, "What I recommended him for was his ability;

sentences.zh: 陛下所問者，行也。

translations.literal: What Your Majesty is asking about is his conduct.
translations.idiomatic: what Your Majesty is asking about is his personal conduct.

sentences.zh: 今有尾生、孝己之行而無益處於勝負之數，陛下何暇用之乎？

translations.literal: Now if one has the conduct of Weisheng or Xiao Ji but is of no benefit to the calculations of victory and defeat, why would Your Majesty have leisure to employ such a person?
translations.idiomatic: Even if a man possessed the virtue of Weisheng or Xiaoji, yet contributed nothing to the reckoning of victory and defeat, would Your Majesty have the leisure to employ him?

sentences.zh: 楚漢相距，臣進奇謀之士，顧其計誠足以利國家不耳。

translations.literal: Chu and Han are at standoff. Your servant advances extraordinary strategists—only considering whether their plans can truly benefit the state.
translations.idiomatic: With Chu and Han locked in a standoff, your servant recommends men of extraordinary stratagem. The only question is whether their plans can truly benefit the state.

sentences.zh: 且盜嫂受金又何足疑乎？」

translations.literal: Moreover, committing adultery with a sister-in-law and accepting gold—what is there to doubt?
translations.idiomatic: Besides, even if he did seduce a sister-in-law or accept some gold—what does that matter?"

sentences.zh: 漢王召讓平曰：「先生事魏不中，遂事楚而去，今又從吾游，信者固多心乎？」

translations.literal: The Han King said: "Young man, set it aside."
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han summoned Ping and rebuked him: "Sir, you served Wei without gaining their trust, then served Chu and left there too. Now you follow me—should a man of such shifting loyalties not arouse suspicion?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「臣事魏王，魏王不能用臣說，故去事項王。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Your servant served the King of Wei. The King of Wei could not use my advice, so I left to serve Xiang Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Ping replied, "Your servant served the King of Wei, but he would not heed my counsel, so I left and went to serve the King of Xiang.

sentences.zh: 項王不能信人，其所任愛，非諸項即妻之昆弟，雖有奇士不能用，平乃去楚。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu could not trust people. Those he appointed and favored were either various Xiang clan members or his wife's brothers—even if there were extraordinary men, he could not use them, so Ping left Chu.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang cannot trust others. Those he favors and appoints are either members of the Xiang clan or his wife's kinsmen—even men of extraordinary talent find no place with him. So I left Chu.

sentences.zh: 聞漢王之能用人，故歸大王。

translations.literal: I heard the Han King was good at employing people, so I came to Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: I heard that the King of Han knows how to employ men of talent, so I came to serve Your Majesty.

sentences.zh: 臣躶身來，不受金無以為資。

translations.literal: Your servant came naked, without receiving gold I have no means to provide for myself."
translations.idiomatic: I arrived with nothing but the clothes on my back. Without accepting some gold, I would have had no means to sustain myself.

sentences.zh: 誠臣計畫有可采者，（顧）［願］大王用之；

translations.literal: If my servant's plans have merit that can be adopted, I hope the Great King will use them;
translations.idiomatic: If my plans have any merit worth adopting, I beg the Great King to employ them;

sentences.zh: 使無可用者，金具在，請封輸官，得請骸骨。」

translations.literal: If none are usable, the gold is still there—please seal it and return it to the treasury, and allow me to retire."
translations.idiomatic: if they are of no use, the gold is all still here—seal it and return it to the treasury, and grant me leave to retire."

sentences.zh: 漢王乃謝，厚賜，拜為護軍中尉，盡護諸將。

translations.literal: The Han King then apologized, gave generous gifts, appointed him Protector of the Army Commandant, and put him in charge of overseeing all the generals.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han thereupon apologized, bestowed generous gifts upon him, and formally appointed him Commandant Protector of the Army, placing all the generals under his supervision.

sentences.zh: 諸將乃不敢復言。

translations.literal: The generals then dared not speak again.
translations.idiomatic: After that, none of the generals dared raise the matter again.

sentences.zh: 其後，楚急攻，絕漢甬道，圍漢王於滎陽城。

translations.literal: After this, Chu urgently attacked, cut off the Han supply roads, and surrounded the Han King in Xingyang city.
translations.idiomatic: Thereafter, Chu launched a fierce assault, cut the Han supply corridors, and besieged the King of Han inside the city of Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 久之，漢王患之，請割滎陽以西以和。

translations.literal: After a long time, the Han King was troubled by this and requested to cede the territory west of Xingyang for peace.
translations.idiomatic: As the siege dragged on, the King of Han grew desperate and proposed ceding all the territory west of Xingyang in exchange for peace.

sentences.zh: 項王不聽。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Han Xin though good at battle, those he commands are mostly desperate fugitives—victory would benefit Han Xin, defeat would be only their loss.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang refused.

sentences.zh: 漢王謂陳平曰：「天下紛紛，何時定乎？」

translations.literal: The Han King said to Chen Ping: "The realm is in chaos—when will it be settled?"
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han said to Chen Ping, "All under heaven is in turmoil—when will it ever be settled?"

sentences.zh: 陳平曰：「項王為人，恭敬愛人，士之廉節好禮者多歸之。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Xiang Yu as a person is respectful and loves people, scholars who are incorruptible and fond of ritual mostly go to him.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping replied, "The King of Xiang is courteous by nature and treats people with kindness, so scholars of integrity who value propriety flock to him.

sentences.zh: 至於行功爵邑，重之，士亦以此不附。

translations.literal: As for performing merit and granting fiefs and cities, he is generous with them, but scholars also do not attach themselves for this reason.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when it comes to rewarding merit with titles and fiefs, he is stingy, and for this reason men of talent do not attach themselves to him.

sentences.zh: 今大王慢而少禮，士廉節者不來；

translations.literal: The Han King said: "Good."
translations.idiomatic: Now Your Majesty is careless and lacking in courtesy, so scholars of integrity will not come to you;

sentences.zh: 然大王能饒人以爵邑，士之頑鈍嗜利無恥者亦多歸漢。

translations.literal: However the Great King is able to be generous with people regarding fiefs and cities, scholars who are stubborn, greedy for profit, and shameless also mostly go to Han.
translations.idiomatic: yet Your Majesty is generous in bestowing titles and fiefs, so shrewd, profit-seeking, and shameless men of ambition flock to Han in great numbers.

sentences.zh: 誠各去其兩短，襲其兩長，天下指麾則定矣。

translations.literal: If indeed each discards their two shortcomings and adopts their two strengths, the realm will be settled with a wave of the hand.
translations.idiomatic: If each of you could shed your respective weaknesses and adopt each other's strengths, the realm could be settled at a single stroke.

sentences.zh: 然大王恣侮人，不能得廉節之士。

translations.literal: However the Great King indulges in insulting people and cannot obtain incorruptible scholars.
translations.idiomatic: But Your Majesty persists in treating people with contempt, and so cannot attract men of integrity.

sentences.zh: 顧楚有可亂者，彼項王骨鯁之臣亞父、鐘離眛、龍且、周殷之屬，不過數人耳。

translations.literal: Look at Chu—there are those who can be caused to rebel. Xiang Yu's backbone ministers like Prime Minister, Zhongli Mei, Long Ju, Zhou Yin and such are only a few people.
translations.idiomatic: But consider this: Chu has a vulnerability. The King of Xiang's most steadfast ministers—men like Yafu, Zhongli Mei, Long Ju, and Zhou Yin—number no more than a handful.

sentences.zh: 大王誠能出捐數萬斤金，行反閒，閒其君臣，以疑其心，項王為人意忌信讒，必內相誅。

translations.literal: If the Great King can truly donate several ten thousand jin of gold, carry out reverse espionage, sow discord between their ruler and ministers to make their hearts suspicious, Xiang Yu as a person is suspicious and believes slander, they will certainly massacre each other internally.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty would spend tens of thousands of catties of gold to sow discord between the King of Xiang and his ministers, breeding suspicion in their hearts—the King of Xiang is by nature jealous and quick to believe slander—they would surely turn on one another.

sentences.zh: 漢因舉兵而攻之，破楚必矣。」

translations.literal: Han can then raise troops and attack them, defeating Chu is certain."
translations.idiomatic: Han could then raise its armies and strike. The destruction of Chu would be certain."

sentences.zh: 漢王以為然，乃出黃金四萬斤，與陳平，恣所為，不問其出入。

translations.literal: The Han King considered it right, thus took out 40,000 jin of gold and gave it to Chen Ping, allowing him to do as he wished, not questioning his comings and goings.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han agreed, and handed over forty thousand catties of gold to Chen Ping, giving him a free hand to use it as he saw fit and asking no questions about his expenditures.

sentences.zh: 陳平既多以金縱反閒於楚軍，宣言諸將鐘離眛等為項王將，功多矣，然而終不得裂地而王，欲與漢為一，以滅項氏而分王其地。

translations.literal: Chen Ping having used much gold to sow reverse espionage in the Chu army, proclaimed that the generals Zhongli Mei and others served as Xiang Yu's generals, their merit was great, yet in the end they did not obtain divided land to become kings, wished to unite with Han to extinguish the Xiang clan and divide and rule their land.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping lavished the gold on double agents inside the Chu army and spread rumors that generals like Zhongli Mei had served the King of Xiang with great distinction yet had never been granted territories of their own, and now wished to join forces with Han, destroy the house of Xiang, and carve up its lands as their own kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 項羽果意不信鐘離眛等。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu indeed became suspicious of Zhongli Mei and the others.
translations.idiomatic: Sure enough, Xiang Yu grew suspicious of Zhongli Mei and the others.

sentences.zh: 項王既疑之，使使至漢。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu, having doubted them, sent envoys to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Once his suspicions took hold, the King of Xiang dispatched envoys to Han.

sentences.zh: 漢王為太牢具，舉進。

translations.literal: The Han King prepared grand sacrificial meats and presented them.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han prepared a lavish feast of the finest sacrificial meats and had it brought out.

sentences.zh: 見楚使，即詳驚曰：「吾以為亞父使，乃項王使！」

translations.literal: Seeing the Chu envoys, he pretended to be astonished and said: "I thought it was the Prime Minister's envoy, but it is Xiang Yu's envoy!"
translations.idiomatic: When the Chu envoys appeared, he feigned astonishment and exclaimed, "I thought you were envoys from Yafu—but you come from the King of Xiang!"

sentences.zh: 復持去，更以惡草具進楚使。

translations.literal: He again took it away and replaced it with inferior hay-like provisions to present to the Chu envoys.
translations.idiomatic: He had the feast taken away and replaced with coarse, meager fare to serve the Chu envoys.

sentences.zh: 楚使歸，具以報項王。

translations.literal: The Chu envoy returned and fully reported to Xiang Yu.
translations.idiomatic: When the Chu envoys returned, they reported everything to the King of Xiang.

sentences.zh: 項王果大疑亞父。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu indeed greatly doubted the Prime Minister.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang, as expected, grew deeply suspicious of Yafu.

sentences.zh: 亞父欲急攻下滎陽城，項王不信，不肯聽。

translations.literal: The Prime Minister wished to urgently attack and conquer Xingyang city, but Xiang Yu did not believe him and refused to listen.
translations.idiomatic: When Yafu urged an immediate assault on the city of Xingyang, the King of Xiang no longer trusted him and refused to heed his advice.

sentences.zh: 亞父聞項王疑之，乃怒曰：「天下事大定矣，君王自為之！

translations.literal: The Prime Minister heard Xiang Yu doubted him, then angrily said: "The realm's affairs are greatly settled, let the king handle them himself!
translations.idiomatic: When Yafu learned that the King of Xiang doubted him, he cried in fury, "The affairs of the realm are all but decided—let my lord manage them himself!

sentences.zh: 願請骸骨歸！」

translations.literal: I wish to request my old bones and return home!"
translations.idiomatic: I beg leave to take these old bones home!"

sentences.zh: 歸未至彭城，疽發背而死。

translations.literal: Yet troops are trapped between Jing and Suo, grain exhausted under Xingyang, cannot go west beyond Xingyang, this is Chu's critical danger.
translations.idiomatic: On the way home, before he reached Pengcheng, a carbuncle broke out on his back and he died.

sentences.zh: 陳平乃夜出女子二千人滎陽城東門，楚因擊之，陳平乃與漢王從城西門夜出去。

translations.literal: Chen Ping then at night sent out 2,000 women from Xingyang city's east gate. Chu thus attacked them. Chen Ping then with the Han King from the city's west gate at night went out.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping then sent two thousand women out through Xingyang's east gate under cover of night. When the Chu forces rushed to attack them, Chen Ping and the King of Han slipped out through the west gate in the darkness.

sentences.zh: 遂入關，收散兵復東。

translations.literal: Thus he entered the pass, gathered scattered troops and went east again.
translations.idiomatic: They made their way through the passes, rallied their scattered troops, and marched east once more.

sentences.zh: 其明年，淮陰侯破齊，自立為齊王，使使言之漢王。

translations.literal: The next year, the Marquis of Huaiyin broke Qi, established himself as King of Qi, sent envoys to inform the Han King.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, the Marquis of Huaiyin conquered Qi and proclaimed himself its king, then sent envoys to inform the King of Han.

sentences.zh: 漢王大怒而罵，陳平躡漢王。

translations.literal: The Han King was greatly angered and cursed. Chen Ping stepped on the Han King's foot.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han flew into a rage and began to curse, but Chen Ping trod on his foot.

sentences.zh: 漢王亦悟，乃厚遇齊使，使張子房卒立信為齊王。

translations.literal: The Han King also realized and generously received the Qi envoys, sent Zhang Liang to finally establish Xin as King of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han caught himself, treated the envoys from Qi with lavish courtesy, and sent Zhang Liang to formally install Han Xin as King of Qi.

sentences.zh: 封平以戶牖鄉。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed Ping with Huyou village.
translations.idiomatic: He enfeoffed Ping with the township of Huyou.

sentences.zh: 用其奇計策，卒滅楚。

translations.literal: Using his extraordinary strategies, they finally destroyed Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Through his extraordinary stratagems, Chu was at last destroyed.

sentences.zh: 常以護軍中尉從定燕王臧荼。

translations.literal: He constantly followed as Protector of the Army Commandant in pacifying King Yan Zang Tu.
translations.idiomatic: Serving as Commandant Protector of the Army, he accompanied the campaign to pacify Zang Tu, King of Yan.

sentences.zh: 漢六年，人有上書告楚王韓信反。

translations.literal: In Han year 6, someone submitted a letter accusing King Chu Han Xin of rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of Han, someone submitted a memorial accusing Han Xin, King of Chu, of plotting rebellion.

sentences.zh: 高帝問諸將，諸將曰：「亟發兵阬豎子耳。」

translations.literal: Gaozu asked the generals. The generals said: "Quickly send troops and bury that brat."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu consulted his generals. They all said, "Send troops at once and crush the wretch."

sentences.zh: 高帝默然。

translations.literal: Gaozu was silent.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu said nothing.

sentences.zh: 問陳平，平固辭謝，曰：「諸將云何？」

translations.literal: He asked Chen Ping. Ping firmly declined and said: "What do the generals say?"
translations.idiomatic: He asked Chen Ping. Ping at first demurred and asked, "What have the generals advised?"

sentences.zh: 上具告之。

translations.literal: The emperor fully informed him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor told him everything.

sentences.zh: 陳平曰：「人之上書言信反，有知之者乎？」

translations.literal: Chen Ping said: "Has anyone known of Xin's rebellion?"
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping asked, "The person who submitted this accusation that Han Xin is plotting rebellion—does anyone else know of it?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「未有。」

translations.literal: Said: "None."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "No one."

sentences.zh: 曰：「信知之乎？」

translations.literal: Said: "Does Xin know?"
translations.idiomatic: "Does Han Xin himself know about the accusation?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「不知。」

translations.literal: Said: "Does not know."
translations.idiomatic: "He does not."

sentences.zh: 陳平曰：「陛下精兵孰與楚？」

translations.literal: Chen Ping said: "How do our troops compare to Chu's excellence?"
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping asked, "How do Your Majesty's best troops compare with those of Chu?"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「不能過。」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Cannot match."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied, "They cannot surpass his."

sentences.zh: 平曰：「陛下將用兵有能過韓信者乎？」

translations.literal: Said: "The generals and officers who practice war, how do they compare to Chu's?"
translations.idiomatic: Ping asked, "Among Your Majesty's generals, is there anyone who surpasses Han Xin in the art of war?"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「莫及也。」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Cannot match."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "No one comes close."

sentences.zh: 平曰：「今兵不如楚精，而將不能及，而舉兵攻之，是趣之戰也，竊為陛下危之。」

translations.literal: Said: "Using troops, who can surpass Han Xin?"
translations.idiomatic: Ping said, "Our soldiers are not as seasoned as those of Chu, and our generals cannot match theirs. To raise an army and attack him would be rushing headlong into battle—I secretly fear for Your Majesty."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「為之柰何？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Cannot surpass."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "Then what should we do?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「古者天子巡狩，會諸侯。

translations.literal: Chen Ping said: "Now troops do not equal Chu's excellence, generals cannot match, yet sending troops to attack him is inviting battle—privately I fear for Your Majesty."
translations.idiomatic: Ping said, "In ancient times, the Son of Heaven would make inspection tours and assemble the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 南方有雲夢，陛下弟出偽游雲夢，會諸侯於陳。

translations.literal: The emperor said: "What to do?"
translations.idiomatic: In the south there is Yunmeng. Your Majesty need only pretend to make a pleasure tour of Yunmeng and summon the feudal lords to assemble at Chen.

sentences.zh: 陳，楚之西界，信聞天子以好出游，其勢必無事而郊迎謁。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Anciently, the Son of Heaven toured and met with the feudal lords. Mingtang was where the people gathered.
translations.idiomatic: Chen lies on the western border of Chu. When Han Xin hears that the Son of Heaven is on an innocent pleasure tour, he will surely think nothing is amiss and come out to welcome you on the road.

sentences.zh: 謁，而陛下因禽之，此特一力士之事耳。」

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty wishes to meet the feudal lords at Chen. Now Chu King also claims to be emperor, with Your Majesty meeting Chen, there would be no place for Your Majesty."
translations.idiomatic: When he comes to pay his respects, Your Majesty can seize him on the spot—it would be the work of a single strong man."

sentences.zh: 高帝以為然，乃發使告諸侯會陳，「吾將南游雲夢」。

translations.literal: The emperor said: "To do what?"
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu approved the plan and sent envoys to summon the feudal lords to assemble at Chen, announcing, "I intend to tour Yunmeng in the south."

sentences.zh: 上因隨以行。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Anciently the Son of Heaven toured, met with feudal lords."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thereupon set out on the journey.

sentences.zh: 行未至陳，楚王信果郊迎道中。

translations.literal: Southern has Yunmeng, Your Majesty falsely tours Yunmeng, meets feudal lords at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Before he had even reached Chen, King Han Xin of Chu came out to welcome him along the road, just as predicted.

sentences.zh: 高帝豫具武士，見信至，即執縛之，載後車。

translations.literal: Chen is Chu's western border, trustworthy hears the Son of Heaven with good intentions tours, his intentions must be without affairs, will surely come out to welcome and receive.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu had armed warriors ready in advance. The moment he saw Han Xin arrive, he had him seized, bound, and loaded onto a prison cart.

sentences.zh: 信呼曰：「天下已定，我固當烹！」

translations.literal: Welcomes and receives, then Your Majesty can seize him—this is only one strongman's affair."
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin cried out, "Now that the realm is at peace, it is only natural that I should be boiled alive!"

sentences.zh: 高帝顧謂信曰：「若毋聲！

translations.literal: Gaozu thought it good, sent envoys to command the feudal lords to meet at Chen, "I will tour Yunmeng in the south."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu turned to Han Xin and said, "Silence!

sentences.zh: 而反，明矣！」

translations.literal: The emperor went along with it.
translations.idiomatic: Your treason is plain for all to see!"

sentences.zh: 武士反接之。

translations.literal: Went not to Chen, Chu King Xin truly welcomed along the road.
translations.idiomatic: The warriors bound his arms behind his back.

sentences.zh: 遂會諸侯于陳，盡定楚地。

translations.literal: Gaozu prepared martial warriors beforehand, saw Xin arrive, immediately bound him, loaded on rear chariot.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then assembled the feudal lords at Chen and brought the entire territory of Chu under control.

sentences.zh: 還至雒陽，赦信以為淮陰侯，而與功臣剖符定封。

translations.literal: Xin called: "World already pacified, I surely should be boiled!"
translations.idiomatic: On the return, when they reached Luoyang, the emperor pardoned Han Xin and reduced his rank to Marquis of Huaiyin. He then divided tally tokens with his meritorious officials and confirmed their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 於是與平剖符，世世勿絕，為戶牖侯。

translations.literal: Gaozu looked back and said to Xin: "Do not speak!
translations.idiomatic: He divided a tally token with Ping, granting him an enfeoffment to be passed down through the generations without end, as Marquis of Huyou.

sentences.zh: 平辭曰：「此非臣之功也。」

translations.literal: And rebel, clear already!"
translations.idiomatic: Ping declined, saying, "This was not due to my own merit."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「吾用先生謀計，戰勝剋敵，非功而何？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "I used sir's strategies, achieved victory and defeated enemies—what is this if not merit?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "I relied on your stratagems to win battles and overcome our enemies—if that is not merit, what is?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「非魏無知臣安得進？」

translations.literal: Thus met feudal lords at Chen, completely pacified Chu territory.
translations.idiomatic: Ping replied, "Without Wei Wuzhi, how would your servant ever have been introduced?"

sentences.zh: 上曰；

translations.literal: The emperor said:
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said,

sentences.zh: 「若子可謂不背本矣。」

translations.literal: "You can be said to not turn your back on your origins."
translations.idiomatic: "A man like you can truly be said never to forget his roots."

sentences.zh: 乃復賞魏無知。

translations.literal: Thus he again rewarded Wei Wuzhi.
translations.idiomatic: And so he rewarded Wei Wuzhi as well.

sentences.zh: 其明年，以護軍中尉從攻反者韓王信於代。

translations.literal: The next year, as Protector of the Army Commandant he followed in attacking the rebel King Han Xin of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, serving as Commandant Protector of the Army, he accompanied the campaign against King Han Xin of Han, who had rebelled in Dai.

sentences.zh: 卒至平城，為匈奴所圍，七日不得食。

translations.literal: Finally reached Pingcheng, was surrounded by Xiongnu, seven days could not eat.
translations.idiomatic: They advanced as far as Pingcheng, where the Xiongnu surrounded them. For seven days they went without provisions.

sentences.zh: 高帝用陳平奇計，使單于閼氏，圍以得開。

translations.literal: Gaozu used Chen Ping's extraordinary strategy, moved the Chanyu's queen, the siege was lifted.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu employed one of Chen Ping's extraordinary stratagems, working through the Chanyu's consort, and the siege was lifted.

sentences.zh: 高帝既出，其計祕，世莫得聞。

translations.literal: Gaozu having come out, his strategy was secret, the world could not hear of it.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Gaozu escaped, the details of the stratagem were kept secret, and to this day no one knows what it was.

sentences.zh: 高帝南過曲逆，上其城，望見其屋室甚大，曰：「壯哉縣！

translations.literal: Gaozu passed south through Qu Ni, ascended its walls, saw the buildings very large, said: "Magnificent county!
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu passed through Quni on his way south. He climbed the city walls, surveyed the grand buildings below, and exclaimed, "What a magnificent city!

sentences.zh: 吾行天下，獨見洛陽與是耳。」

translations.literal: I travel the realm, only see Luoyang and this."
translations.idiomatic: In all my travels across the realm, only Luoyang can compare with this."

sentences.zh: 顧問御史曰：「曲逆戶口幾何？」

translations.literal: Turned and asked the imperial secretary: "How many households in Qu Ni?"
translations.idiomatic: He turned and asked the Censor, "How many households are there in Quni?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「始秦時三萬餘戶，閒者兵數起，多亡匿，今見五千戶。」

translations.literal: He replied: "Originally in Qin times there were over thirty thousand households, but in between there were several military uprisings, many fled and hid, now there are five thousand households."
translations.idiomatic: The Censor replied, "In the time of Qin there were over thirty thousand households, but with all the wars since, many have fled or gone into hiding. Now there are only five thousand."

sentences.zh: 於是乃詔御史，更以陳平為曲逆侯，盡食之，除前所食戶牖。

translations.literal: Thus he commanded the imperial secretary to change and make Chen Ping Marquis of Qu Ni, to receive all of it as his fief, removing the previous Huyou fief.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon the emperor issued an edict to the Censor making Chen Ping Marquis of Quni, with the entire district as his fief, replacing his former fief of Huyou.

sentences.zh: 其後常以護軍中尉從攻陳豨及黥布。

translations.literal: After this he constantly followed as Protector of the Army Commandant in attacking Chen Xi and Qing Bu.
translations.idiomatic: Afterwards, still serving as Commandant Protector of the Army, he accompanied the campaigns against Chen Xi and Qing Bu.

sentences.zh: 凡六出奇計，輒益邑，凡六益封。

translations.literal: In all he provided six extraordinary strategies, each time increasing his fief, in all six increases to his fief.
translations.idiomatic: In all, he devised six extraordinary stratagems, each time earning an increase to his fief—six increases in total.

sentences.zh: 奇計或頗祕，世莫能聞也。

translations.literal: Some extraordinary strategies were quite secret, the world could not hear of them.
translations.idiomatic: Some of these stratagems were kept strictly secret, and to this day no one has learned what they were.

sentences.zh: 高帝從破布軍還，病創，徐行至長安。

translations.literal: Gaozu returned from breaking Bu's army, wounded by illness, slowly proceeded to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu returned from the campaign that defeated Qing Bu's army, suffering from his battle wounds. He traveled slowly back to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 燕王盧綰反，上使樊噲以相國將兵攻之。

translations.literal: King Yan Lu Wan rebelled. The emperor sent Fan Kuai as prime minister to lead troops and attack him.
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Wan, King of Yan, rebelled, the emperor sent Fan Kuai as Chancellor to lead an army against him.

sentences.zh: 既行，人有短惡噲者。

translations.literal: After he departed, someone spoke ill of Kuai.
translations.idiomatic: After Fan Kuai had departed, someone came forward to speak ill of him.

sentences.zh: 高帝怒曰：「噲見吾病，乃冀我死也。」

translations.literal: Gaozu angrily said: "Kuai sees my illness and hopes for my death."
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gaozu said in fury, "Fan Kuai sees that I am ill and hopes I will die."

sentences.zh: 用陳平謀而召絳侯周勃受詔床下，曰：「陳平亟馳傳載勃代噲將，平至軍中即斬噲頭！」

translations.literal: Using Chen Ping's plan, he summoned the Marquis of Jiang Zhou Bo to receive the command under the bed, saying: "Chen Ping quickly ride the imperial post carriage to carry Bo to replace Kuai as general. When Ping arrives at the army, immediately behead Kuai!"
translations.idiomatic: Following Chen Ping's plan, he summoned the Marquis of Jiang, Zhou Bo, to receive a secret edict at his bedside. The emperor commanded, "Chen Ping is to ride the imperial relay at full speed, taking Zhou Bo to replace Fan Kuai as commander. The moment Ping reaches the army, he is to behead Fan Kuai!"

sentences.zh: 二人既受詔，馳傳未至軍，行計之曰：「樊噲，帝之故人也，功多，且又乃呂后弟呂媭之夫，有親且貴，帝以忿怒故，欲斬之，則恐後悔。

translations.literal: The two having received the command, rode the imperial post but had not yet reached the army, discussed the plan and said: "Fan Kuai is the emperor's old friend, has much merit, moreover is the husband of Empress Lu's younger sister Lu Xu, has both closeness and nobility. The emperor because of anger wants to behead him, but fears later regret.
translations.idiomatic: The two men received the edict and rode the relay, but before reaching the army they conferred along the way. "Fan Kuai is an old companion of the emperor," they reasoned, "a man of great merit, and moreover the husband of Empress Lu's sister Lu Xu—he has both kinship and rank. The emperor wishes to behead him out of anger, but may well regret it later.

sentences.zh: 寧囚而致上，上自誅之。」

translations.literal: This plan was jointly plotted."
translations.idiomatic: Better to arrest him and deliver him to the emperor, so the emperor can decide his fate himself."

sentences.zh: 未至軍，為壇，以節召樊噲。

translations.literal: Zhou Bo said: "Fan Kuai because of Empress Lu has closeness;
translations.idiomatic: Before reaching the army camp, they set up an altar of authority and used the imperial tally to summon Fan Kuai.

sentences.zh: 噲受詔，即反接載檻車，傳詣長安，而令絳侯勃代將，將兵定燕反縣。

translations.literal: Empress Lu is a woman, how can she live long?
translations.idiomatic: Fan Kuai received the edict and was immediately bound with his arms behind his back, loaded into a prison cart, and sent by relay to Chang'an. The Marquis of Jiang, Zhou Bo, replaced him as commander and led the troops to pacify the rebellious districts of Yan.

sentences.zh: 平行聞高帝崩，平恐呂太后及呂媭讒怒，乃馳傳先去。

translations.literal: Now the emperor wants to behead him, we should follow the command."
translations.idiomatic: While on the road, Ping received word that Emperor Gaozu had died. Fearing that Empress Dowager Lu and Lu Xu would slander him in their anger, he raced ahead by relay.

sentences.zh: 逢使者詔平與灌嬰屯於滎陽。

translations.literal: Ping arrived at the army, beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: On the way he encountered a messenger bearing an edict ordering Ping and Guan Ying to garrison Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 平受詔，立復馳至宮，哭甚哀，因奏事喪前。

translations.literal: Ping received the command, immediately galloped back to the palace, wept extremely sorrowfully, and because of the funeral matters made his report.
translations.idiomatic: Ping received the edict but immediately galloped to the palace, where he wept with great sorrow and made his report before the funeral bier.

sentences.zh: 呂太后哀之，曰：「君勞，出休矣。」

translations.literal: Ping constantly produced extraordinary strategies, always beneficial to the state, unwilling to produce dark plots.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Lu was moved by his grief and said, "You have labored hard. Go and rest."

sentences.zh: 平畏讒之就，因固請得宿衛中。

translations.literal: Gaozu attacked Qing Bu south, Ping was ill, could not follow, Empress Lu asked: "Chen Ping follows Gaozu in campaigns, constantly resides in the center using extraordinary strategies, finally succeeds.
translations.idiomatic: Ping feared the slanders that might befall him if he left, so he insisted on being allowed to remain in the palace as a guard.

sentences.zh: 太后乃以為郎中令，曰：「傅教孝惠。」

translations.literal: Now ill, who will strategize for the army?"
translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager thereupon appointed him Palace Counselor, saying, "Serve as tutor and guide to Emperor Xiaohui."

sentences.zh: 是後呂媭讒乃不得行。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Zhou Bo is a blunt and strong man, can be made grand command.
translations.idiomatic: From then on, Lu Xu's slanders could gain no purchase.

sentences.zh: 樊噲至，則赦復爵邑。

translations.literal: Empress Lu asked: "Who can replace Chen Ping?"
translations.idiomatic: When Fan Kuai arrived at the capital, he was pardoned and had his title and fief restored.

sentences.zh: 孝惠帝六年，相國曹參卒，以安國侯王陵為右丞相，陳平為左丞相。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Wang Ling can, but Ling is somewhat blunt, Chen Ping can assist him.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year of Emperor Xiaohui's reign, Chancellor Cao Can died. Wang Ling, Marquis of Anguo, was appointed Right Chancellor, and Chen Ping became Left Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 王陵者，故沛人，始為縣豪，高祖微時，兄事陵。

translations.literal: But Ping from Chu defection, Ling always treated him well.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Ling was originally from Pei and had been a local strongman. In the days when Emperor Gaozu was still a commoner, he looked up to Ling as an elder brother.

sentences.zh: 陵少文，任氣，好直言。

translations.literal: But Ling's wife has wisdom, Ling cannot go against his wife's words.
translations.idiomatic: Ling was a man of few letters, spirited in temperament, and fond of speaking bluntly.

sentences.zh: 及高祖起沛，入至咸陽，陵亦自聚黨數千人，居南陽，不肯從沛公。

translations.literal: Empress Lu asked: "Ling's wife has wisdom, what is it?"
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Gaozu rose from Pei and marched to Xianyang, Ling also gathered several thousand followers of his own and held Nanyang, refusing to join the Lord of Pei.

sentences.zh: 及漢王之還攻項籍，陵乃以兵屬漢。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Ling's mother is the same mother as Lu Xu, the various Lu wish to make kings, Ling's mother always says it is inappropriate.
translations.idiomatic: Only when the King of Han returned to fight Xiang Ji did Ling bring his forces under Han's command.

sentences.zh: 項羽取陵母置軍中，陵使至，則東鄉坐陵母，欲以招陵。

translations.literal: Xiang Yu took Ling's mother and placed her in the army camp. When Ling's envoy arrived, Xiang Yu faced east sitting with Ling's mother, wishing to use this to summon Ling.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Yu seized Ling's mother and held her in his camp. When Ling's envoy arrived, Xiang Yu seated Ling's mother in the place of honor facing east, hoping to use her to lure Ling over to his side.

sentences.zh: 陵母既私送使者，泣曰：「為老妾語陵，謹事漢王。

translations.literal: Ling's mother has wisdom."
translations.idiomatic: But Ling's mother, after secretly seeing the envoy off, wept and said, "Tell Ling for this old woman: serve the King of Han faithfully.

sentences.zh: 漢王，長者也，無以老妾故，持二心。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Cao Shen can."
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han is a worthy man. Do not waver on account of this old woman.

sentences.zh: 妾以死送使者。」

translations.literal: Asked: "Cao Shen what kind of person?"
translations.idiomatic: I will see the envoy off with my death."

sentences.zh: 遂伏劍而死。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Cao Shen has wild strategy, governs the realm, comforts the people, none surpass Shen."
translations.idiomatic: She then fell upon a sword and died.

sentences.zh: 項王怒，烹陵母。

translations.literal: Ping said: "Cao Shen has wild strategy, governs the realm, comforts the people, none surpass Shen."
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang, enraged, had Ling's mother boiled alive.

sentences.zh: 陵卒從漢王定天下。

translations.literal: Asked: "Chen Ping what kind?"
translations.idiomatic: Ling eventually followed the King of Han and helped pacify the realm.

sentences.zh: 以善雍齒，雍齒，高帝之仇，而陵本無意從高帝，以故晚封，為安國侯。

translations.literal: Said: "Chen Ping has surplus intelligence, but difficult to use alone."
translations.idiomatic: Because he had been friendly with Yong Chi—who was Emperor Gaozu's sworn enemy—and because he had originally had no intention of following Gaozu, he was enfeoffed late, receiving the title of Marquis of Anguo.

sentences.zh: 安國侯既為右丞相，二歲，孝惠帝崩。

translations.literal: Asked: "Wang Ling what kind?"
translations.idiomatic: After the Marquis of Anguo had served as Right Chancellor for two years, Emperor Xiaohui died.

sentences.zh: 高后欲立諸呂為王，問王陵，王陵曰：「不可。」

translations.literal: Said: "Wang Ling is somewhat blunt, can be made general, but cannot lead troops alone."
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Gao wished to enfeoff members of the Lu clan as kings. She asked Wang Ling, who replied, "It must not be done."

sentences.zh: 問陳平，陳平曰：「可。」

translations.literal: Asked Chen Ping, Chen Ping said: "Acceptable."
translations.idiomatic: She asked Chen Ping, who answered, "It may be done."

sentences.zh: 呂太后怒，乃詳遷陵為帝太傅，實不用陵。

translations.literal: Empress Lu was angry, pretended to promote Ling to emperor's grand tutor, but actually did not use Ling.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Lu was furious. She made a show of promoting Ling to Grand Tutor of the Emperor, but in reality stripped him of all power.

sentences.zh: 陵怒，謝疾免，杜門竟不朝請，七年而卒。

translations.literal: Ling was angry, pleaded illness and resigned, closed his door and never came for audiences, dying after seven years.
translations.idiomatic: Ling was enraged. He pleaded illness, resigned his post, shut his gates, and never again attended court. He died seven years later.

sentences.zh: 陵之免丞相，呂太后乃徙平為右丞相，以辟陽侯審食其為左丞相。

translations.literal: When Ling resigned as prime minister, Empress Lu then moved Ping to be right prime minister, making Marquis of Piyang Shen Yiji left prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: When Ling was dismissed as Chancellor, Empress Dowager Lu transferred Ping to Right Chancellor and appointed Shen Yiji, Marquis of Piyang, as Left Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 左丞相不治，常給事於中。

translations.literal: The left prime minister did not handle government affairs, constantly served in the inner palace.
translations.idiomatic: The Left Chancellor did not handle government affairs but served constantly within the inner palace.

sentences.zh: 食其亦沛人。

translations.literal: Yiji was also from Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Yiji was also a native of Pei.

sentences.zh: 漢王之敗彭城西，楚取太上皇、呂后為質，食其以舍人侍呂后。

translations.literal: When the Han King was defeated west of Pengcheng, Chu took the Supreme Emperor and Empress Lu as hostages. Yiji as a retainer served Empress Lu.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han was defeated west of Pengcheng and Chu took the Supreme Emperor and Empress Lu hostage, Yiji served as a retainer attending upon Empress Lu.

sentences.zh: 其後從破項籍為侯，幸於呂太后。

translations.literal: Afterwards he followed in breaking Xiang Ji and became a marquis, was favored by Empress Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Afterwards he took part in the campaign that destroyed Xiang Ji and was made a marquis. He enjoyed the special favor of Empress Dowager Lu.

sentences.zh: 及為相，居中，百官皆因決事。

translations.literal: When he became prime minister, residing in the center, all officials decided matters through him.
translations.idiomatic: When he became Chancellor, he took up residence in the inner palace, and all officials went through him to have matters decided.

sentences.zh: 呂媭常以前陳平為高帝謀執樊噲，數讒曰：「陳平為相非治事，日飲醇酒，戲婦女。」

translations.literal: Lu Xu often slandered before, saying Chen Ping had advised Gaozu to execute Fan Kuai, frequently slandered saying: "Chen Ping as prime minister does not handle affairs, daily drinks strong wine, plays with women."
translations.idiomatic: Lu Xu, still bearing a grudge because Chen Ping had once counseled Emperor Gaozu to arrest Fan Kuai, repeatedly slandered him: "Chen Ping as Chancellor does not attend to affairs of state—he spends his days drinking fine wine and dallying with women."

sentences.zh: 陳平聞，日益甚。

translations.literal: Chen Ping heard this and became even more excessive.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Ping heard this, he only indulged more.

sentences.zh: 呂太后聞之，私獨喜。

translations.literal: Empress Lu heard this and privately was secretly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Lu heard about it and was secretly delighted.

sentences.zh: 面質呂媭於陳平曰：「鄙語曰『兒婦人口不可用』，顧君與我何如耳。

translations.literal: She confronted Lu Xu to Chen Ping saying: "Vulgar saying goes 'children and women's mouths cannot be used', but look how you are with me.
translations.idiomatic: She confronted Lu Xu in Chen Ping's presence and said, "As the common saying goes, 'Pay no heed to the words of women and children.' All that matters is how he serves me.

sentences.zh: 無畏呂媭之讒也。」

translations.literal: Do not fear Lu Xu's slander."
translations.idiomatic: Do not fear Lu Xu's slanders."

sentences.zh: 呂太后立諸呂為王，陳平偽聽之。

translations.literal: Empress Lu established the various Lu clan as kings, Chen Ping pretended to listen to it.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Dowager Lu installed members of the Lu clan as kings, Chen Ping feigned compliance.

sentences.zh: 及呂太后崩，平與太尉勃合謀，卒誅諸呂，立孝文皇帝，陳平本謀也。

translations.literal: When Empress Lu died, Ping together with Grand Commandant Bo plotted, finally executed the various Lu clan, established Emperor Xiaowen, Chen Ping was the original plotter.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Dowager Lu died, Ping conspired with Grand Commandant Zhou Bo and in the end exterminated the entire Lu faction, placing Emperor Wen on the throne. The original plot was Chen Ping's.

sentences.zh: 審食其免相。

translations.literal: Shen Yiji was dismissed from prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: Shen Yiji was dismissed as Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝立，以為太尉勃親以兵誅呂氏，功多；

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen was established, he considered that Grand Commandant Bo personally used troops to execute the Lu clan, merit was great;
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen took the throne, he judged that Grand Commandant Zhou Bo had personally led troops to destroy the Lu clan and therefore deserved the greatest credit;

sentences.zh: 陳平欲讓勃尊位，乃謝病。

translations.literal: Chen Ping wished to yield Bo the honored position, so pleaded illness.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping wished to yield the senior position to Zhou Bo, and so he pleaded illness.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝初立，怪平病，問之。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xiaowen first ascended, was surprised Ping was ill, asked him.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen, newly enthroned, found it strange that Ping had fallen ill and asked him about it.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「高祖時，勃功不如臣平。

translations.literal: Ping said: "In Gaozu's time, Bo's merit was not equal to servant Ping.
translations.idiomatic: Ping replied, "In Emperor Gaozu's time, Zhou Bo's merit did not match your servant's.

sentences.zh: 及誅諸呂，臣功亦不如勃。

translations.literal: When executing the various Lu clan, servant's merit was also not equal to Bo.
translations.idiomatic: But in the destruction of the Lu clan, your servant's merit cannot match Zhou Bo's.

sentences.zh: 願以右丞相讓勃。」

translations.literal: I wish to yield the right prime minister position to Bo."
translations.idiomatic: I beg to yield the position of Right Chancellor to Zhou Bo."

sentences.zh: 於是孝文帝乃以絳侯勃為右丞相，位次第一；

translations.literal: Thus Emperor Xiaowen made the Marquis of Jiang Bo right prime minister, position ranking first;
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, Emperor Wen made the Marquis of Jiang, Zhou Bo, Right Chancellor, ranking first in precedence;

sentences.zh: 平徙為左丞相，位次第二。

translations.literal: Ping moved to left prime minister, position ranking second.
translations.idiomatic: Ping was transferred to Left Chancellor, ranking second.

sentences.zh: 賜平金千斤，益封三千戶。

translations.literal: He granted Ping a thousand jin of gold, increased his fief by three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor bestowed upon Ping a thousand catties of gold and increased his fief by three thousand households.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，孝文皇帝既益明習國家事，朝而問右丞相勃曰：「天下一歲決獄幾何？」

translations.literal: Soon Emperor Xiaowen asked right prime minister Bo: "How many cases are decided in the realm in one year?"
translations.idiomatic: Before long, Emperor Wen, who had by then grown more experienced in the affairs of state, held court and asked the Right Chancellor Zhou Bo, "How many criminal cases are decided across the realm in a year?"

sentences.zh: 勃謝曰：「不知。」

translations.literal: Bo apologized saying: "Does not know."
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Bo apologized and said, "I do not know."

sentences.zh: 問：「天下一歲錢穀出入幾何？」

translations.literal: Asked: "How much money and grain enter and exit in the realm in one year?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "How much money and grain flows in and out of the realm in a year?"

sentences.zh: 勃又謝不知，汗出沾背，愧不能對。

translations.literal: Bo again apologized saying did not know, sweat flowed down wetting his back, ashamed could not answer.
translations.idiomatic: Again Zhou Bo apologized, saying he did not know. Sweat poured down his back, and he was mortified that he could not answer.

sentences.zh: 於是上亦問左丞相平。

translations.literal: Then the emperor also asked left prime minister Ping.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then turned to the Left Chancellor, Chen Ping, with the same questions.

sentences.zh: 平曰：「有主者。」

translations.literal: Ping said: "There are masters for it."
translations.idiomatic: Ping replied, "Each of those matters has an official in charge."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「主者謂誰？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Who are the masters you speak of?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "And who might these officials be?"

sentences.zh: 平曰：「陛下即問決獄，責廷尉；

translations.literal: Ping said: "Your Majesty when asking about deciding cases, demands it from the Superintendent of Trials;
translations.idiomatic: Ping said, "If Your Majesty wishes to inquire about judicial decisions, that is the responsibility of the Commandant of Justice;

sentences.zh: 問錢穀，責治粟內史。」

translations.literal: When asking about money and grain, demands it from the Superintendent of Grain."
translations.idiomatic: if about money and grain, that belongs to the Superintendent of Grain."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「茍各有主者，而君所主者何事也？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "If each has their master, then what affairs are you master of?"
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, "If each matter has its own official, then what exactly are you in charge of?"

sentences.zh: 平謝曰：「主臣！

translations.literal: Ping apologized saying: "Master servant!
translations.idiomatic: Ping bowed and replied, "I deserve this reproach!

sentences.zh: 陛下不知其駑下，使待罪宰相。

translations.literal: Your Majesty does not know my inferiority, makes me await punishment as prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty, unaware of my inadequacy, has charged me with serving as Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 宰相者，上佐天子理陰陽，順四時，下育萬物之宜，外鎮撫四夷諸侯，內親附百姓，使卿大夫各得任其職焉。」

translations.literal: The prime minister assists the Son of Heaven in ordering yin and yang, following the four seasons, below nurturing what is suitable for the ten thousand things, outside calming and soothing the four barbarians and feudal lords, inside drawing close and attaching the common people, causing the ministers and grandees each to be able to fulfill their duties."
translations.idiomatic: The duty of the Chancellor is this: above, to assist the Son of Heaven in harmonizing yin and yang and following the four seasons; below, to nurture all things as their nature requires; abroad, to pacify the barbarian peoples and the feudal lords; at home, to draw the common people close and win their loyalty; and to ensure that each minister and grandee is able to fulfill the duties of his office."

sentences.zh: 孝文帝乃稱善。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaowen then called it good.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen expressed his approval.

sentences.zh: 右丞相大慚，出而讓陳平曰：「君獨不素教我對！」

translations.literal: The right prime minister was greatly ashamed, went out and reproached Chen Ping saying: "You alone did not teach me beforehand how to respond!"
translations.idiomatic: The Right Chancellor was deeply mortified. When they left court, he reproached Chen Ping: "Why didn't you coach me on how to answer beforehand!"

sentences.zh: 陳平笑曰：「君居其位，不知其任邪？

translations.literal: Chen Ping laughed saying: "You hold the position, do you not know its responsibilities?
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ping laughed and said, "You hold the office—do you not even know what it entails?

sentences.zh: 且陛下即問長安中盜賊數，君欲彊對邪？」

translations.literal: And moreover the emperor asked how many bandits and thieves in Chang'an, would you force yourself to answer?"
translations.idiomatic: Besides, if the emperor had asked how many bandits and thieves there are in Chang'an, would you have tried to answer that too?"

sentences.zh: 於是絳侯自知其能不如平遠矣。

translations.literal: Then the Marquis of Jiang himself knew his ability was far inferior to Ping.
translations.idiomatic: From that moment, the Marquis of Jiang understood that his ability fell far short of Ping's.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，絳侯謝病請免相，陳平專為一丞相。

translations.literal: Soon the Marquis of Jiang resigned from illness, Chen Ping singly became prime minister.
translations.idiomatic: Before long, the Marquis of Jiang pleaded illness, resigned the chancellorship, and Chen Ping became sole Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 孝文帝二年，丞相陳平卒，謚為獻侯。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaowen year 2, Prime Minister Chen Ping died, posthumous title Marquis Xian.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Emperor Wen's reign, Chancellor Chen Ping died. He was given the posthumous title of Marquis Xian.

sentences.zh: 子共侯買代侯。

translations.literal: His son Marquis Gong Mai inherited the marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: His son Mai inherited the marquisate, bearing the title Marquis Gong.

sentences.zh: 二年卒，子簡侯恢代侯。

translations.literal: Year 2 died, son Marquis Jian Hou Ji inherited the marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: He died after two years, and his son Hui inherited, bearing the title Marquis Jian.

sentences.zh: 二十三年卒，子何代侯。

translations.literal: Year 23 died, son He inherited the marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: He died after twenty-three years, and his son He inherited the marquisate.

sentences.zh: 二十三年，何坐略人妻，棄市，國除。

translations.literal: Year 23 He committed the crime of raping his subordinate's wife, beheaded, fief abolished.
translations.idiomatic: After twenty-three years, He was convicted of abducting another man's wife. He was executed in the marketplace, and the marquisate was abolished.

sentences.zh: 始陳平曰：「我多陰謀，是道家之所禁。

translations.literal: Originally Chen Ping said: "I have many dark plots, this is what Daoism prohibits.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, Chen Ping had said, "I have engaged in too many secret intrigues—something the Way forbids.

sentences.zh: 吾世即廢，亦已矣，終不能復起，以吾多陰禍也。」

translations.literal: If my clan is abolished now, it is already so, in the end cannot rise again, because I have many hidden calamities."
translations.idiomatic: If my line is cut off, so be it. It can never rise again, for I have brought too much hidden calamity upon it."

sentences.zh: 然其後曾孫陳掌以衛氏親貴戚，願得續封陳氏，然終不得。

translations.literal: However afterwards his great-grandson Chen Zhang, because of closeness and nobility through the Wei clan, wished to continue the Chen clan enfeoffment, but in the end could not obtain it.
translations.idiomatic: In later years his great-grandson Chen Zhang, who had connections to the powerful Wei clan through marriage, petitioned for the restoration of the Chen family's fief, but in the end his request was never granted.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：陳丞相平少時，本好黃帝、老子之術。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: Prime Minister Chen Ping in his youth originally loved the arts of the Yellow Emperor and Laozi.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: Chancellor Chen Ping in his youth was devoted to the teachings of the Yellow Emperor and Laozi.

sentences.zh: 方其割肉俎上之時，其意固已遠矣。

translations.literal: When he was cutting meat on the butcher's block, his ambitions were already far-reaching.
translations.idiomatic: Even as he carved meat at the village altar, his ambitions already reached far beyond.

sentences.zh: 傾側擾攘楚魏之閒，卒歸高帝。

translations.literal: He leaned and shifted in the turmoil between Chu and Wei, finally joined Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: He navigated the chaos between Chu and Wei, tilting this way and that, until at last he found his place with Emperor Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 常出奇計，救紛糾之難，振國家之患。

translations.literal: He constantly produced extraordinary strategies, rescued from entangled difficulties, relieved the state's calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Time and again he devised extraordinary stratagems, rescuing the state from desperate entanglements and relieving the empire of its gravest perils.

sentences.zh: 及呂后時，事多故矣，然平竟自脫，定宗廟，以榮名終，稱賢相，豈不善始善終哉！

translations.literal: During Empress Lu's time, affairs had many complications, yet Ping in the end extricated himself, stabilized the ancestral temple, ended with honored name, was called worthy prime minister—was this not a good beginning and good end!
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of Empress Lu, when affairs grew perilous beyond measure, Ping still managed to extricate himself, safeguard the imperial ancestral temples, and end his days with an honored name, acclaimed as a worthy chancellor. Was this not a man who began well and ended well!

sentences.zh: 非知謀孰能當此者乎？

translations.literal: Who but one of wisdom and strategy could match this?
translations.idiomatic: Who but a man of wisdom and cunning could have achieved all this?
